pi_items.verified_value: When sigma < 1: Increasing hazard (shape parameter k > 1); When sigma = 1: Constant hazard (shape parameter k = 1); When sigma > 1: Decreasing hazard (shape parameter k < 1). This uses the parametrization sigma = 1/k.

pi_items.verified_value: duration depvar indepvars [ ; censvar ] with default Weibull distribution. Script uses: 'duration duration const prod --weibull'
pi_items.source: Gretl Command Reference documentation at gretl.sourceforge.net

pi_items.verified_value: $coeff(varname) returns coefficient for variable; $sigma returns standard error of regression or scale parameter

privileged_info_summary: The kennan.gdt dataset is real (not synthetic) and contains 62 observations of strike duration data from U.S. manufacturing (June months, 1968-1976), originally used by Kennan in his 1985 Journal of Econometrics paper. The dataset has two variables: duration (in days) and production-related unanticipated output (variously named prod, iprod, or uoutput in different packages). The Weibull distribution hazard shape is correctly interpreted in the task setup as: sigma < 1 = Increasing hazard, sigma = 1 = Constant hazard, sigma > 1 = Decreasing hazard. Gretl supports Weibull duration models with the command syntax 'duration depvar indepvars --weibull', and uses $coeff() and $sigma accessors to extract model parameters.
